text: Unlike chemists who need to devise a special synthesis to gain access to an architecturally complex natural product, in Nature, chemists (biosynthetic enzymes) can start with a relatively simple molecule (such as a common precursor polyunsaturated intermediate) and use a variety of divergent intramolecular processes (such as multiple types of intramolecular Diels-Alder reactions and intramolecular reductive couplings) to give many different structural scaffolds. In an effort to marry the efficiency of natural biosynthetic pathways to the power of chemical synthesis, we demonstrated the preparation of an artificial divergent heterocyclic polyketomacrocycle assembly line that enables rapid access to an unprecedented number of analogs of terpenoid indole alkaloids with diverse skeletal configurations, extensive variations of stereocenters, and varying number and types of functional groups. Access to multipotent intermediates as well as a flexible route to highly divergent analogs are achieved without requiring any structural simplification to known natural products. Our [4+2] cycloadditions which were achieved through three distinct mechanisms: as formal hetero-Diels-Alder, hetero-[4+2] annulation between two unsaturated heteroaromatics, or hetero-homo-[4+2] upon reacting an unsaturated heteroaromatic with a heteroatomic olefin; as well as the two redox-mediated intramolecular hetero-annulations at unsaturated heteroaromatic precursors afforded five distinct skeletal configurations corresponding to iboga-, aspidosperma-, andranginine-, and ngouniensine-type skeletons as well as one novel unnatural skeletal configuration. Syntheses with yields ranging from 45 to 85% took 6-9 steps from tryptamine depending on the target product. Syntheses of (±)-vincadifformine, (±)-andranginine, and (−)-catharanthine are illustrated. Natural products generally display the following characteristics that impart to them their bioactivities: a wide variety of functional groups, on the relatively rigid and complex (aromatic and sp3) three-dimensional structural framework. These structural characteristics enables a natural product to recognize with high affinity a large number of bio-macromolecules by making contacts with several of their critical structural features. This allows for the optimization of drug candidate lead structures in short order through the efficient access of large numbers of synthetic analogs through proven multiple routes.
